author: nflaig
content: <@808969530608451584> I didn't understand what you meant? do we wanna discuss this here maybe or rather on a PR/github

author: pk910
content:
yea sure 🙂   it's basically this change: https://github.com/wemeetagain/EIPs/commit/e04626092adffa461e2d6c9303f217e22918169e
Instead of hard-coding how many entries are dequeued, we pass it through the system call.
That'd allow us the adjust the rate in a later fork without re-deploying the contract.

author: pk910
content:
and <@360491619402776577> rel re-deployment: 
I think we should never re-deploy a contract that is in active use. Especially if it's used as a deposit contract with an actual value.
The contract address will likely be hard coded in other contracts or builder toolings, so re-deploying & changing the public address at a fork boundary should really be the very last option.

author: nflaig
content: but the max itself isn't entirely that relevant? can you do the same for the target?

author: nflaig
content: but sounds like a net improvement as it adds flexiblitity we may use later, no comment on the complexity it adds, leaving that up to EL devs

author: pk910
content:
need to check what's the current status on the per-tx fee.  because that likely touches the same code parts.
but yea,  ideally we should set both,   but the target likely needs to be persisted in storage to be available for the fee calculation :/
